macro_events.event_summary: Trump administration reportedly in advanced talks for a federal rescue package for bankrupt/distressed Spirit Airlines.
primary_drivers: Government bailout reduces Spirit bankruptcy/liquidation risk, keeping ULCC capacity in the market
Capacity preservation pressures yields for competing carriers that would have absorbed Spirit's routes
Creditor/equity recovery improves for existing Spirit stakeholders

beneficiaries.rationale: JetBlue has deep operational and historical M&A ties to Spirit (prior blocked merger); a stabilized Spirit reduces disorderly capacity dumping on overlapping Florida/Caribbean/Northeast routes where JBLU competes directly. Also removes overhang of a distressed competitor fire-saling fares.

losers.rationale: Frontier is Spirit's closest ULCC peer and was a prior failed acquirer. A government-backed Spirit remains a full-strength low-fare competitor on overlapping leisure routes (Vegas, Orlando, Cancun), preventing Frontier from absorbing Spirit's share and pricing power. Frontier loses the most from Spirit NOT disappearing.

Allegiant competes with Spirit on ultra-low-fare leisure routes to Florida and Vegas; a rescued Spirit means continued fare competition rather than route/capacity consolidation that would have lifted ALGT yields.
Southwest overlaps Spirit on many domestic leisure markets; had been positioned to pick up abandoned gates/slots at FLL, MCO, LAS in a liquidation. Rescue removes that capacity-grab optionality.

American has major hubs (MIA, DFW, CLT) with Spirit overlap, particularly in Florida and Latin America leisure. Would have been a beneficiary of Spirit capacity reduction — rescue preserves the discount competition on those routes.

headlines: Trump administration in advanced talks for a rescue package for Spirit Airlines, source says

macro_events.event_summary: McDonald's UK reportedly plans to raise menu prices citing cost pressures tied to the Iran conflict, signaling commodity/energy cost pass-through into QSR pricing.
primary_drivers: Higher energy and shipping costs from Middle East conflict flowing into food service input costs
QSR pricing power / menu inflation pass-through
Oil price premium on geopolitical risk

beneficiaries.rationale: Direct read-through: MCD is executing price increases to protect margin against input cost inflation. Franchise-heavy model (~95% franchised) means royalty revenue scales with ticket price while corporate bears little direct commodity risk.
Iran conflict sustaining an oil risk premium is the underlying driver MCD is citing; integrated majors with large upstream exposure capture the price lift directly.
Same mechanism as XOM — Middle East risk premium on crude supports upstream realizations; no direct Iranian exposure to lose.
Peer QSR (Burger King, Tim Hortons) facing identical UK/European input cost pressure will likely follow with price increases, similar franchise-royalty mechanic as MCD.

losers.rationale: Company-operated (non-franchised) model means Chipotle absorbs food and energy inflation directly on the P&L rather than passing via royalty; has historically been more reluctant to raise prices aggressively. Sympathy read on QSR cost-pressure narrative.
Wendy's has weaker pricing power than MCD and lower international scale; rising beef and energy costs pressure franchisee margins and unit economics if they can't match MCD's price hikes.
Airlines are the cleanest negative read on sustained oil premium from Iran conflict — jet fuel is ~25-30% of operating cost and JetBlue has weaker hedging and thinner margins than legacy peers.
Southwest unwound its fuel hedges and is fully exposed to spot jet fuel; the same Iran-driven crude premium MCD is citing flows straight to LUV's cost line.

headlines: McDonald’s UK plans to raise prices due to Iran conflict – report

macro_events.event_summary: Bitcoin breaks out to fresh highs, driving crypto-linked equities like MicroStrategy (+9%) and Marathon Digital (+6%) sharply higher.
primary_drivers: Bitcoin spot price appreciation directly marks up BTC treasury holdings and miner revenue per coin
Higher BTC price increases miner gross margins (fixed hashcost, variable revenue)
Crypto beta rotation lifts exchanges, brokers, and BTC-proxy equities

beneficiaries.rationale: Holds ~500k+ BTC on balance sheet; equity trades as a leveraged BTC proxy with premium to NAV expanding on breakouts. Already cited in headline as +9%.
Largest public BTC miner by hashrate; revenue scales linearly with BTC price while cash cost per coin is relatively fixed, producing outsized EPS sensitivity. Cited in headline.
Pure-play BTC miner with significant self-mined BTC treasury; high operating leverage to spot BTC means it typically outperforms MARA on up-moves with similar mechanics.
US-based BTC miner with Texas operations; peer-correlated to MARA/CLSK and typically moves in lockstep on BTC breakouts due to identical revenue model.
Transaction revenue directly tied to crypto trading volumes, which spike on BTC breakouts; also holds corporate crypto on balance sheet. Retail flow catalyst on new highs.
Crypto trading now a material (~20%+) revenue contributor; BTC breakouts historically drive retail options and crypto volume surges through the platform.
BTC miner with HPC/AI hosting optionality; smaller-cap miner that tends to get squeezed hard on BTC momentum days alongside WULF, IREN.

headlines: Strategy Jumps 9%, Marathon Digital Rises 6% on Fresh Bitcoin Breakout

macro_events.event_summary: Small and medium businesses are abandoning wait-and-see posture and actively overhauling supply chains in response to sustained tariffs, implying a wave of sourcing shifts, nearshoring, and logistics spend.
primary_drivers: Supply chain reconfiguration spend (consulting, software, logistics)
Nearshoring/reshoring demand shift away from China
Working capital strain and inventory rebalancing for import-dependent SMBs

beneficiaries.rationale: Contract logistics pure-play directly benefits when shippers re-route supply chains; reconfiguration drives new 3PL contract wins and warehouse redesign work.
Contract manufacturer with Mexico and Southeast Asia footprint — primary beneficiary of SMB/mid-market OEMs moving production out of China. Direct revenue channel for 'China+1' moves.
Core & Main and domestic industrial distributors capture share as SMBs shift to US-sourced inputs; distribution margin expands in reshoring cycle.
Shopify's Markets/Logistics and supplier network tools are the software layer SMBs use to re-source; platform captures both new-supplier onboarding and cross-border compliance revenue.
Expeditors' non-asset freight forwarding earns more on complex, re-routed trade lanes (Vietnam/Mexico/India) than on stable China-US direct lanes — complexity is their margin.

losers.rationale: ZIM is heavily exposed to the trans-Pacific China-US container lane; SMB re-routing to Mexico/Vietnam shrinks the specific lane where ZIM is overweight.

Five Below imports roughly 60% of COGS from China at sub-$5 price points where tariff pass-through is nearly impossible; supply chain overhaul implies sustained margin compression, not a transient hit.
Dollar General's private-label and discretionary mix relies on low-cost Chinese sourcing; SMB-style supply chain shifts at DG's scale are slower and costlier, pressuring gross margin.
Etsy sellers are the archetypal SMB caught in this overhaul — many source components from China. Friction, cost inflation, and seller churn pressure GMV.

headlines: SMBs ditch ‘wait-and-see’ as tariffs force supply chain overhaul
